app.name: SlowQL
app.one_liner: A production-focused offline SQL static analyzer that detects security vulnerabilities, performance regressions, reliability issues, compliance risks, cost inefficiencies, and code quality problems without requiring a live database connection.

screens.name: CLI Dashboard
screens.purpose: Display analysis results, rule violations, and remediation options in an interactive terminal interface.

rust_commands.name: analyze_sql
rust_commands.purpose: Perform static analysis on provided SQL input using built-in rules and optional DDL schema context.

rust_commands.name: apply_autofix
rust_commands.purpose: Safely apply automated fixes for supported anti-patterns with diff preview and backup support.

rust_commands.name: export_report
rust_commands.purpose: Generate and export analysis results in specified formats (SARIF, JSON, GitHub annotations).

mvp_plan: week 1: Add --diff and --fix modes with backup support
week 1: Build CLI interface supporting multi-file input
week 1: Implement core offline SQL parser with source anchoring
week 1: Integrate 171 built-in rules across six categories
week 2: Add GitHub Actions and pre-commit hook support
week 2: Develop severity threshold and --fail-on logic
week 2: Implement SARIF and JSON output formatters
acceptance_tests: Given a SQL file with NULL comparison anti-pattern, when analyzed with SlowQL, then it should report QUAL-NULL-001 violation and offer safe autofix.
Given a SQL query referencing non-existent table, when DDL schema is provided, then it should flag missing table error.
Given multiple SQL files in a directory, when run with --fix --diff, then it should show preview diffs without modifying files.
When executed in CI with --fail-on error, and high-severity issue found, then exit code should be non-zero.
